revision.text:
[[ja:伊我理神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Igari Shrine & Inaka Shrine |religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Igari & Inaka-jinja 03.jpg |location= {{ill|Toyokawa-chō (Ise)|ja|豊川町 (伊勢市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyokawa-chō (Ise)|1=Toyokawachō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17223204}}, {{ill|Ise, Mie|en|Ise, Mie|qid=Q328067|lt=Ise, Mie|1=Ise, Mie}}, {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|qid=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}} |latitude_deg = 34 | latitude_min = 29 | latitude_sec = 5.0 |longitude_deg = 136 |longitude_min = 42 | longitude_sec = 27.9 |map= Japan Mie |deity= Igari Shrine: Igaribime no Mikoto (伊我利比女命)
Inaka Shrine: Inaka-no-Kami (井中神) |Rank= {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Subordinate Shrine |founded= Before the [[Heian period]] |main_hall= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |other_names= |pilgrimage_sites= |annual_festival= |rituals= |prefecture= {{ill|Toyokawachō|qid=Q17223204|1=Toyokawachō}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.484722|N|136.70775|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |architecture_style= {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Igari Shrine''' (伊我理神社, *Igari Jinja*) is a {{ill|Setsumatsusha|en|Setsumatsusha|qid=Q1071160|lt=subordinate shrine (sessha/massha)|1=Setsumatsusha}} of {{ill|Toyōke Daijingū|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (the Outer Shrine of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}). It is located within the sacred forest of the Outer Shrine. This article also describes the adjacent '''Inaka Shrine''' (井中神社, *Inaka Jinja*), which is another subordinate shrine of Toyōke Daijingū, enshrined alongside Igari ShrineIse Bunka-sha (2008): p.38. == Overview == {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igari Shrine|qq=}} and {{ill|Igari Shrine|ja|伊我理神社|lt=Inaka Shrine|lt_ja=井中神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igari Shrine|qq=}} are located within the precinct forest (Jingu Forest) of the Outer Shrine (Geku) of Ise Grand Shrine in Toyokawa Town, Ise City, Mie Prefecture. However, they are not directly connected to the main approach of the Outer Shrine. The shrine buildings appear as you proceed along the perimeter of the shrine forest. Among the eight subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine, Igari Shrine Ranks first, and Inaka Shrine Ranks third. The precincts are densely surrounded by large trees, making it dark even during the daytime. The shrine buildings are enclosed by sacred fences (tamagaki). A large camphor tree stands beside the shrine buildings. There are no offering boxes present. In front of Igari Shrine and Inaka Shrine is the {{ill|Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine|ja|度会大国玉比賣神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine|qq=}}, an auxiliary shrine of the Outer Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == Igari Shrine enshrines {{ill|Igarihime-no-Mikoto|ja|伊我利比女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Igarihime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, while Inaka Shrine enshrines {{ill|Inaka-no-Kami|ja|井中神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaka-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The name "Igarihime-no-Mikoto" derives from "inoshishi-gari" (boar hunting), and she is a goddess who hunts wild boars that ravage five grains. Such deities were enshrined because the Outer Shrine owned sacred rice fields known as "Toyomiyazaki no Kanda" until the mid-Showa period. Inaka-no-Kami is considered the deity of the sacred well in these rice fields. == History == The exact founding date is unknown. The subordinate shrines of the Outer Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine are not listed in the "{{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}" and were not part of state rituals at that time. However, these shrines were officially recognized under the jurisdiction of the Watarai Shrine in the early Heian period, suggesting that they existed at least by that time. Fragments and rims of earthenware have been found on the shrine grounds, most of which are {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}. == Access == === To the Outer Shrine Front === * About a 5-minute walk through the Outer Shrine approach from the south exit (JR side) of Ise City Station on the JR Sangu Line and Kintetsu Yamada Line. * About a 5-minute walk from the "Ise City Station Front" bus stop of {{ill|Mie Kotsu|en|Mie Kotsu|qid=Q6843516|lt=Mie Kotsu|1=Mie Kotsu}}. * Immediately accessible from the "Outer Shrine Front" bus stop of Mie Kotsu. * About a 5-minute drive north from Ise Nishi IC on the Ise Expressway via Mie Prefectural Route 32 (Mikimoto Road). Parking is available nearby. === From the Outer Shrine Front to the Shrines === Proceed south along Mikimoto Road, adjacent to the precinct forest (Jingu Forest), from the Outer Shrine Front. Turn left and go straight at the site of the former Toyomiya-zaki Library. Ascend the moss-covered stone steps to reach the shrine building of Watarai Okunitama-hime Shrine. Ascend another flight of stone steps to see Igari Shrine and Inaka Shrine. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * 伊勢市 編『伊勢市史 第六巻 考古編』伊勢市、平成23rd year 3月31日、821pp. * 櫻井勝之進『伊勢神宮 <改訂新版>』[[学生社]]、1998th year 4月25日、237pp. ISBN 4-31140714-9 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、平成20th year 12月23日、151pp. ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4 == See Also == * [[神社一覧]] == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m02/0215.html 伊我理神社（いがりじんじゃ） 御同座 井中神社] - 財団法人伊勢神宮崇敬会 {{125 Shrines of Ise}} {{translated page|ja|伊我理神社|version=65885348|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:伊我理神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11379372}} [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Setsumatsusha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 100+ untranslated japanese characters]]
